<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Documents cover memory architecture, PRD stewardship, and level-system reference — these are high-value artifacts for onboarding and architectural governance
- Several files reference 'memory' concerns (memory-architecture.md, memory-improvements.md, memory-os-behavior.md, memory-risks-and-flaws.md), suggesting active design work on a memory subsystem worth tracking as a formal PRD feature
- Zero coupling is ideal for a docs zone; no production code should import from here
- Four memory-focused documents indicate a concentrated area of active design; consider consolidating into a single reference doc once decisions stabilize to reduce maintenance burden.
- The 2026-03-03 date-prefixed analysis file suggests a growing set of dated design notes; a lightweight ADR (Architecture Decision Record) convention would make this history easier to navigate.
- Perfect cohesion and zero coupling are correct — documentation should never be a module dependency, and this zone achieves that cleanly.
- The architecture-documentation zone is the only zone with a date-prefixed file (2026-03-03-refresh-orchestration-memory-analysis.md) alongside undated files — this mixed convention means the zone has two incompatible organizational schemes for the same category of content, making chronological vs. topical retrieval ambiguous.
- Standardize documentation file naming: either date-prefix all analysis/decision files (ADR convention) or use no date prefixes and rely on git history. The current mix of one dated file among undated peers creates an inconsistent retrieval experience and will worsen as the docs zone grows.

</insights>
